template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS CHILD SUPPORT GUIDELINES WORKSHEET**:
Formal calculation of the presumptive child support order under the Massachusetts Child Support Guidelines (effective October 4, 2021, Trial Court of the Commonwealth), required by Probate and Family Court Rule 411 to accompany any complaint, modification, or agreement involving child support. Presents gross income, adjustments, allowable deductions, combined available income, and the resulting presumptive order. Target length: 2–4 pages plus exhibits.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `numberOfChildren`: integer (1–5+). Guidelines contain a different multiplier table for each count.
- `parentingTimeSplit`: `primary_non_primary` (default — one parent has >2/3 parenting time) | `shared_physical` (each parent has between 1/3 and 2/3 parenting time, triggering cross-guidelines calculation) | `split_custody` (one child with each parent, rare).
- `deviationRequested`: boolean — when true, include a deviation-justification section addressing the factors in Guidelines § IV.
- `combinedIncomeAboveCap`: boolean — flags when combined available income exceeds $400,000/year, requiring additional discretion analysis under Guidelines § II.D and Cavanagh v. Cavanagh, 490 Mass. 398 (2022).

**STRUCTURE**:

## CHILD SUPPORT GUIDELINES WORKSHEET

### PARTIES AND CHILDREN
- **Recipient parent (Parent A)**: [name]
- **Payor parent (Parent B)**: [name]
- **Child(ren)**: [name(s) with DOB(s)]
- **Parenting time split**: [describe per `parentingTimeSplit`]
- **Guidelines effective**: October 4, 2021 edition (Trial Court of the Commonwealth, promulgated pursuant to M.G.L. c. 208, § 28 and 42 U.S.C. § 667)

### STEP 1 — GROSS WEEKLY INCOME (each parent)
Identify gross income from ALL sources, consistent with Guidelines § I.A (Sources of Income):
- Salary, wages, tips, commissions, bonuses (include the last 12 months of bonus/commission as historical average)
- Self-employment net income after ordinary and necessary business expenses
- Overtime and part-time income where recurring
- Rental income (net of operating expenses)
- Interest, dividends, capital gains (annualized)
- Retirement, pension, Social Security (including Social Security dependent benefits received on child's behalf, which are credited against the Guidelines order per Rosenberg v. Merida, 428 Mass. 182 (1998))
- Unemployment and workers' compensation benefits
- Imputed income where a parent is voluntarily underemployed consistent with Guidelines § I.E

| Income Source | Parent A (weekly) | Parent B (weekly) |
|---|---:|---:|
| Base salary | $_____ | $_____ |
| Commissions / bonuses (avg) | $_____ | $_____ |
| Self-employment | $_____ | $_____ |
| Other: [describe] | $_____ | $_____ |
| **GROSS WEEKLY INCOME** | **$_____** | **$_____** |

### STEP 2 — ALLOWABLE DEDUCTIONS
Per Guidelines § I.C, deduct from gross income:
- Child support actually paid to another child under a prior order
- Child care costs attributable to employment or education (up to the cap in Guidelines § II.F)
- Health insurance premiums paid for the child (portion attributable to the child)
- Dental / vision insurance premiums paid for the child

| Deduction | Parent A | Parent B |
|---|---:|---:|
| Prior child support order(s) | $_____ | $_____ |
| Child care (work/education) | $_____ | $_____ |
| Child health insurance (child portion) | $_____ | $_____ |
| **TOTAL DEDUCTIONS** | **$_____** | **$_____** |
| **AVAILABLE WEEKLY INCOME** | **$_____** | **$_____** |

### STEP 3 — COMBINED AVAILABLE INCOME AND GUIDELINES AMOUNT
Add the available weekly income for both parents. Enter the combined figure in the Guidelines' Table for `numberOfChildren`. The table produces the combined presumptive weekly child support amount.

**COMBINED AVAILABLE WEEKLY INCOME**: $_____
**GUIDELINES TABLE AMOUNT (for [N] child(ren))**: $_____

### STEP 4 — PRO-RATA SHARE
Each parent's share of the Guidelines amount is proportional to his or her share of the combined available income.

| Parent | Available Income | Pro-Rata % | Share of Guidelines |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Parent A | $_____ | ____% | $_____ |
| Parent B | $_____ | ____% | $_____ |

### STEP 5 — PRESUMPTIVE ORDER
[Based on `parentingTimeSplit`]:

- **primary_non_primary**: Payor parent's pro-rata share, adjusted for the child-related costs the payor already covers directly, is the presumptive weekly order. Recipient parent receives $_____ per week.

- **shared_physical** (cross-guidelines calculation under Guidelines § II.D.3): Run the Guidelines twice — once with Parent A as payor, once with Parent B as payor — then net the two figures. The parent with the higher Guidelines amount pays the difference.
  - Parent A as payor: Guidelines amount $_____
  - Parent B as payor: Guidelines amount $_____
  - Net presumptive order: Parent [X] pays Parent [Y] $_____ per week.

- **split_custody**: Calculate separately for each child based on which parent has primary physical custody; net the results.

### STEP 6 — HEALTH INSURANCE, UNINSURED, AND CHILD CARE ORDERS
- **Health insurance**: [Parent X] shall maintain health insurance for the child(ren) at reasonable cost, consistent with Guidelines § IV and M.G.L. c. 119A, § 12.
- **Unreimbursed medical expenses**: Parents share unreimbursed medical, dental, vision, orthodontic, and mental-health expenses pro rata in the same percentage as the pro-rata income shares above (Parent A: ____% / Parent B: ____%), after an annual cap of $250 per child paid by the custodial parent (Guidelines § II.I).
- **Child care**: Actual work-related child care costs are added back to the Guidelines order pro rata.

### STEP 7 — [IF deviationRequested] DEVIATION JUSTIFICATION
[When deviation is requested, address the factors in Guidelines § IV]: (a) parties' stipulation to deviation is in the child's best interest; (b) extraordinary medical expenses; (c) extraordinary travel/transportation costs for parenting time; (d) substantial disparity in parenting-time costs; (e) the child's particular needs; (f) other — specifically describe why the presumptive order is unjust or inappropriate.

### STEP 8 — [IF combinedIncomeAboveCap] INCOME ABOVE $400,000
Per Cavanagh v. Cavanagh, 490 Mass. 398 (2022), when combined available income exceeds $400,000, the Court must make a factual finding as to the children's station-in-life needs, and may extrapolate from the Guidelines table in a reasoned manner. Include: analysis of children's needs; parties' lifestyle during the marriage; factors considered in deviation.

### STEP 9 — DURATION AND MODIFICATION
Child support continues until the child is emancipated under M.G.L. c. 208, § 28 and the 2021 Guidelines § III: age 18; until age 21 if domiciled with a parent and principally dependent; until age 23 if enrolled in an undergraduate program. The parties may seek modification under the inconsistency test of M.G.L. c. 208, § 28 (presumption of modification when the existing order differs from Guidelines by more than 20%).

### CERTIFICATION
I certify under the pains and penalties of perjury that the income and expenses reported above are true and accurate to the best of my knowledge, and that this worksheet was prepared in compliance with the Massachusetts Child Support Guidelines (effective October 4, 2021) and Supplemental Probate and Family Court Rule 411.

[Signature line for each party / attorney, dated]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 208, § 28`, `M.G.L. c. 119A, § 12`.
- Cite Guidelines sections exactly: `Guidelines § I.A`, `Guidelines § II.D.3`, `Guidelines § IV`. Use the 2021 edition unless the engagement explicitly specifies an earlier version.
- Cite case law exactly: `Cavanagh v. Cavanagh, 490 Mass. 398 (2022)` and `Rosenberg v. Merida, 428 Mass. 182 (1998)`.
- Cite federal law where applicable: `42 U.S.C. § 667` (Title IV-D requirement that states have Guidelines).
- When `shared_physical` is true, the cross-guidelines calculation is MANDATORY — do not use the single-calculation method.
- When `combinedIncomeAboveCap` is true, the Cavanagh section is MANDATORY.
- Do NOT use figures from older Guidelines editions (2009, 2013, 2018) without clear labeling.
- Round all computations to the nearest dollar.
hints: Massachusetts Child Support Guidelines Worksheet — 2021 edition, required by Supp. P&F Ct. Rule 411. Computes gross income → deductions → combined available → Guidelines table lookup → pro-rata share → presumptive order. Cross-guidelines calculation for shared physical under Guidelines § II.D.3. Cavanagh v. Cavanagh (2022) analysis when combined income > $400K. Rosenberg v. Merida credit for Social Security dependent benefits. 20% inconsistency test for modifications under M.G.L. c. 208, § 28. Age 18/21/23 emancipation thresholds per Guidelines § III.
